subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Rate & Accumulation (Riemann Sums)
stimulus:
The amount of water in a storage tank, in gallons, is modeled by a continuous function on the time interval $0 \le t \le 7$, where $t$ is measured in hours. In this model, rates are given as follows:

(i) The rate at which water enters the tank is $f(t) = 100t^2 \sin t$ gallons per hour for $0 \le t \le 7$.

(ii) The rate at which water leaves the tank is $g(t) = \begin{cases} 250 & 0 \le t < 3 \\ 2000 & 3 < t \le 7 \end{cases}$ gallons per hour.

The graphs of $f$ and $g$, which intersect at $t = 1.617$ and $t = 5.076$, are shown in the figure above. At time $t = 0$, the amount of water in the tank is 5000 gallons.

parts.prompt: How many gallons of water enter the tank during the time interval $0 \le t \le 7$? Round your answer to the nearest gallon.

scoring_points.description: Correct setup and answer: total water entering

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 7$, find the time intervals during which the amount of water in the tank is decreasing. Give a reason for each answer.

scoring_points.description: Correct time intervals when water decreasing

required_elements: The rate leaving exceeds the rate entering on those intervals

scoring_points.description: Complete answer with both intervals and reasons

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 7$, at what time $t$ is the amount of water in the tank greatest? To the nearest gallon, compute the amount of water at this time. Justify your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: Maximum at t=5.076 where f(t)=g(t) and f-g changes from positive to negative. W(5.076) = 5000 + integral_0^{5.076} [f(t)-g(t)] dt ≈ 4976 gallons. Justification: W'(t) = f(t) - g(t) changes from positive to negative at t=5.076.

scoring_points.description: Identifies $t = 5.076$ as location of maximum with reason: $f(t) - g(t)$ changes sign from positive to negative

required_elements: $f(t) > g(t)$ for $1.617 < t < 5.076$ (tank increasing) and $f(t) < g(t)$ for $5.076 < t \le 7$ (tank decreasing)

scoring_points.description: Sets up correct integral expression: $W(5.076) = 5000 + \int_0^{5.076}[f(t)-g(t)]\, dt$

parts.prompt: Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the total amount of water in the tank, in gallons, at time $t = 7$.
parts.scoring_notes: W(7) = 5000 + integral from 0 to 7 of [f(t) - g(t)] dt. Both the initial condition 5000 and the correct net rate integrand are required.

scoring_points.description: Correct net rate integrand $f(t) - g(t)$ in integral from $0$ to $7$

alternatives.correct_example: $\int_0^7 [f(t) - g(t)]\, dt$ with $f(t) = 100t^2\sin t$ and piecewise $g(t)$
